input.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

raw.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

winner: Why was Elphaba always a hit at magic shows? Because she had the electrifying power to save the day with her magnetic performances! (Wicked: For Good)

candidates.text: Why was Glinda expelled from school? Because she kept using her magnetism for bad instead of good, for WICKED!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'magnetism' and 'for good,' ties to the headline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Apparently, the gravity-defying lifts in 'Wicked' were all thanks to the cast's strong magnetism... I guess they really meant it when they said they'd move mountains.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'magnetism' and 'gravity-defying,' specific to the show, concise, and surprising with a clever twist."}

candidates.text: Why was Elphaba always a hit at magic shows? Because she had the electrifying power to save the day with her magnetic performances! (Wicked: For Good)
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'magnetic performances' and 'electrifying power' are clever and specific to the headline, with a natural flow and surprise element."}

candidates.text: Why was Wicked so magnetic at the box office? Because for good it attracted an iron-clad audience!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "Puns on 'magnetic' and 'for good' are clever and specific, but the joke feels slightly forced and the punchline is a bit predictable."}

candidates.text: Why did the witches' production of "For Good" need a magnet? Because Glinda and Elphaba had magnetic personalities!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the magnetic twist, but it's a bit forced and relies on a pun that may not land as well due to lack of setup. It meets constraints but lacks surprise and specificity."}
